figures.caption: Figure 2: Effect of dataset size on training metrics (normalized step). Larger datasets delay memorization onset, reduce memorization rate, and improve peak fidelity.
Figure 3: Effect of model size on training metrics. Larger models exhibit faster memorization onset, and peak fidelity is improved by increased capacity.
Table 1: Common hyperparameters for training and sampling from diffusion models in all the experiments.
Figure 4: Effect of batch size on training metrics (normalized step). Smaller batch sizes improve peak fidelity while leaving the memorization onset and rate largely unchanged.
Figure 5: Effect of learning rate on training metrics (normalized step). Larger learning rates delay the normalized memorization onset and improve peak fidelity.

Figure 1: Predicted versus actual evolution of training metrics. Left: based on the literature (Section 2.2), we expected the train and test loss to decrease together until τmem, at which point the model overfits, and distributional distances to track the loss. Right: actual experiment (N = 2048, P = 92M, B = 16, η = 10−4). The double descent in sliced Wasserstein distance and FID (bottom), present for both train and test, is unexpected.
